ownership_contract.task_goal: Preserve trusted controller handoff across two separately submitted Core read tasks on one confirmed headed managed session.
read_scope: Harbor Runtime Session controller lifecycle, HTTP lifecycle tests, and HARBOR-263 carriers
non_goals: No App/Core/Lode or other Harbor worktree changes
No real browser, account, profile, page, credential, Cookie, token, raw material, or external write
ownership_contract.output_format: Ready implementation PR bound to Harbor #263 for main-controller review and gates.

ownership_constraints: Writes are limited to the registered Harbor #263 worktree and declared runtime-session/test/carrier paths.
App, Core, Lode, other Harbor worktrees, BOSS probe code, secrets, raw runtime material, and external pages are forbidden.
Merge, issue closeout, and packaged live E2E remain main-controller responsibilities.
integration_evidence: A trusted confirmed Core release now re-arms the next explicit Core acquisition only when the prior trusted handoff and matching held lock are present.
The focused lifecycle test proves two separate acquire/probe/release cycles on the same session and preserves the pending handoff against user/agent claims.
Existing negative lifecycle tests cover unconfirmed, headless visibility, fixture, different identity/profile, released controller, user lock, holder conflict, and cleanup failure boundaries.

suite_path_consumption.consumer_boundary: Harbor shared session/controller lifecycle only; site probes and downstream contracts are unchanged.
suite_path_consumption.recheck_condition: Refresh after lifecycle, test, ownership, product head, or PR metadata changes.
